summary: Gruber shares a Keyboard Maestro macro he created to prevent accidentally pasting images into MarsEdit, his blogging app. When he pastes with an image on the clipboard, MarsEdit opens an unwanted Upload Utility window that he must manually dismiss. His macro intercepts ⌘V, checks if the clipboard contains an image, and either blocks the paste (playing a sound) or passes the keystroke through normally. He uses this as a launching point to evangelize Keyboard Maestro as an essential Mac power user tool, noting that creating the macro took far less time than writing about it.
key_points: Keyboard Maestro macros can intercept an app's own keyboard shortcuts, conditionally execute logic, and pass the shortcut through without triggering infinite loops
The macro pattern of if-then-else on clipboard contents is broadly applicable to many app-specific annoyances beyond this particular MarsEdit problem
Keyboard Maestro executes so fast it's invisible to the user, sometimes requiring deliberate pauses to avoid outrunning the UI it's automating
The friction of small recurring annoyances is worth eliminating, and Keyboard Maestro makes the fix often faster than enduring the annoyance one more time
money_quotes: My number one tip for becoming a Mac power user is to get into Keyboard Maestro. Using Keyboard Maestro feels like gaining superpowers.
I've spent 10× more time writing about this macro here than I did creating it.
Keyboard Maestro macros execute so fast that in some scenarios, you have to add steps to pause for, say, 0.2 seconds to keep the macro from getting ahead of the user interface it's manipulating.
Keyboard Maestro lets you effectively add your own little features to your favorite apps — often with no 'scripting' at all.
The best part is, while writing this very blog post, my new 'Don't Paste Images' macro saved me from seeing that cursed Upload Utility window once more.
I bet you have your own esoteric annoyances related to your own most-used apps and most-frequent tasks.
key_insight: Keyboard Maestro transforms minor recurring app annoyances into solved problems so quickly that the fix often takes less effort than suffering through the annoyance one more time.
